params.name: Rajavadiya Dragon Pattern Glazed Tile
params.description: The Raja Vaidya Dragon Pattern Glazed Tile was created during the Ilkhanate period in the 14th century. It features a blue glaze base made of lapis lazuli, using overglaze techniques to depict a unique dragon pattern, vividly showcasing the posture of a dragon turning its head. As an Iranian artifact, it carries the ancient culture of Iran, witnessing the artistic style of the Ilkhanate period. Due to the oriental cultural elements of the dragon pattern, it reflects the exchange and fusion of Ea …
